price_details: site starts photography around $2,500; additional hours run $350/hr, 2nd photographer $150/hr, engagement sessions $500-$1,000 depending on length, albums $750-$2,500, photo booth add-on $700. travel billed at $1/mile round trip to the reception, doubled if you add a 2nd shooter.
notes:
-photojournalistic/documentary style per their site, they also do video and you save $250-500 bundling photo+video+content creation
-reviews name emily & bethany as shooters, both get called out for making couples comfortable in front of the camera
-loved detail: instant sneak peek photo shown at the wedding itself
-a la carte pricing is genuinely itemized on the site, no "call for quote" runaround

price_details: couldn't find any package pricing on their site, it just links out to a separate "Mr & Mrs RICH" site for the actual wedding/elopement photography info. you'd have to inquire directly for rates.
notes:
-ellie and her husband dillon shoot together, reviews describe them as a team on shoots
-she's described as both photographer and former model, which reviewers say shows in how she directs posing
-most of the reviews on google are actually for maternity/family sessions, not weddings specifically, so wedding-day proof is thinner than i'd like
-multiple reviewers mention her editing style giving a soft, polished feel

price_text: starts around $945, hourly add-ons on top
price_details: a regional pricing digest lists wedding packages starting at $945. site itself doesn't break out wedding tiers but lists extras: extra hour $300/operator, drone add-on $500, wireless camera $300, portable power unit $100.
notes:
-heads up, this is a rebrand of "All Digital Photo & Video" and has been around since 1999
-photo AND video together, one review specifically praises paul as the wedding photographer and devin for the video edit
-a chunk of their recent reviews are actually about corporate/commercial event work, not weddings, so weigh the 5 star average accordingly
-drone footage is an option if that's your thing
sources: site /pricing; google reviews; region pricing digest

price_details: the embodied art experience is a flat $2900 plus tax and includes a full day session plus a luxury 8x8 album with 20 images. zero-interest payment plans are available split monthly or biweekly after a deposit.
notes: -this is a boudoir studio (gabby is the photographer named in reviews) not a wedding-day photographer

price_details: site says full pricing requires a phone consult. session fee alone is $500; collections start at $350 and digital image packages start at $650 on top of that. 0% interest payment plans available starting around $50/month.
notes: -boudoir studio not a wedding photographer, ali is named as the photographer in a couple reviews

price_details: site says "please contact for current pricing" and only lists a starting 2 hour session plus $250 for each additional hour past that. wedding packages run 3-10 hours per the site copy but the actual price sheet is on request only.
notes: -no travel fee within 1 hour of boulder per their site\n-they do call out "small weddings & elopements" as a specific offering alongside family/newborn work\n-only 4 google reviews and none are actually about a wedding, they're maternity/newborn/engagement, so treat the wedding side as less proven

price_details: site has zero pricing, just a warm intro line about family albums. no packages, no rates listed anywhere i could find.
notes: -based in fruita/mesa county and every single review i found is family, newborn, or maternity work. couldn't turn up one wedding mention. NOTPHOTOG:emily-o-photography

price_details: site is FAQ-style and just says packages vary by need, no rate sheet, though a la carte funds can roll into a package if you upgrade later.
notes: -this is a florist and month-of wedding planner (caley is named in every review) doing floral design and coordination in estes park, not photography. NOTPHOTOG:fanciful-floral-weddings

price_details: site emphasizes "clear, upfront pricing" as a selling point but the actual numbers aren't posted beyond add-ons: heirloom album $450, polaroid add-on $250 for 8, hair & makeup add-on $300, prints starting at $5.
notes: -boudoir studio (petra is the photographer named across reviews), not wedding-day work. NOTPHOTOG:fawntail-boudoir-photography

price_details: site lists an elopement package starting at $4,000, a full wedding planning package starting at $5,000, and a micro wedding planning package starting at $5,500.
notes: -winnie runs this as an elopement and wedding planning business for colorado and destination couples, not a photography studio, reviews all describe her as their planner. NOTPHOTOG:for-wild-love

price_details: no pricing posted on their site at all, you'd need to reach out directly for rates.
notes:
-emily and her husband alan shoot together as a two-photographer team, reviews mention this repeatedly as a plus since it means fewer missed moments
-reviewers describe their approach as storytelling-driven
-one reviewer got married in colorado while living out of state and still made it work with them
-another mentions shooting through unexpected rain without it derailing the day

price_details: no pricing listed on their site.
notes: -wes runs this and reviews are a mix of engagement/wedding proposal video work alongside straight-up commercial real estate listing videos and drone footage, so it reads more like a videography/drone outfit than a dedicated wedding photographer. NOTPHOTOG:four-corners-digital-imagery

venue: Four Seasons Resort and Residences Vail
price_details: site advertises a curated wedding package but no rates are posted publicly, just up to 20% off room rate when you book your stay in advance.
notes: -this is a luxury hotel/resort with its own wedding package and event space in vail, not a photographer. reviews are all about the stay itself (one 3 star flags pricey/mediocre food, another flags overly frequent housekeeping). NOTPHOTOG:four-seasons-vail

price_details: no pricing posted on their site.
notes:
-shot a wedding at copper mountain per one review, so she covers mountain venues
-reviewers repeatedly mention she and her team make camera-shy couples comfortable
-one review describes her turning a rainy wedding day into a solid gallery anyway
-reviews span 2017-2022, a little dated, so responsiveness/current availability is unverified

price_details: couldn't get their pricing page to load so no rates to share here, you'd have to reach out directly.
notes: -chelsey is the photographer named across reviews\n-does engagement, wedding, and elopement shoots, one review specifically calls out a fall/halloween themed elopement shoot\n-a recurring theme in reviews is she takes real time to learn the couple before the shoot rather than just showing up
